description: Placing per-thread counters or state adjacently in one struct, so cores ping-pong the shared cache line and parallel code does not scale.
body: Failure mode: an array of per-thread counters (or a struct of hot fields written by different cores) lands within a single 64-byte line, so every write invalidates the line in other cores' caches; coherence traffic dominates and adding threads slows the program. Detection signals: throughput flat or falling as threads increase; perf c2c shows HITM / cache-line bouncing on those addresses; each thread's data is logically independent. Do not pack independently-written hot variables into one cache line. Safer fallback: pad each per-thread datum to its own cache line (alignas(64) or spacing) or keep state thread-local and aggregate at the end, then re-measure scaling and HITM events.
